reasoning: The 4-week moving average (CC4WSA) has shown a consistent upward momentum over the last 4 weeks leading up to 2025-10-25 (increasing from 1923.5k to 1947.5k). A 4-week moving average is mechanically auto-correlated; the recent increases indicate that the latest raw weekly claims are running ~30k higher than the weeks dropping out of the calculation. For the MA to decrease by 2025-11-02 (just one week later), the new weekly print would have to abruptly drop by at least 30k from recent levels, which is highly unlikely absent a massive sudden reversal. Therefore, the 1-week projection has a high probability of increase. One month out (2025-11-25), there is more time for the trend to normalize if the recent spike was due to temporary factors, so confidence decreases. Three months out (2026-01-24), typical seasonal adjustments and larger macro shifts make the outcome significantly more uncertain, moving the probability closer to an uninformative prior (55%).
